title: thelypteris puberula
intro: Thelypteris puberula, also known as the hairy maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used in herbal preparations to promote health and well-being, with benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential applications in treating diabetes and high blood pressure. Herbal preparations of Thelypteris puberula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent.
